change_log.description: Initial issuance of the Vendor Onboarding Procedure.

purpose_text: The purpose of this procedure is to define the steps the Workforce follows to qualify, onboard, and activate a new vendor so that every vendor doing business with NextDecade has been assessed for safety, financial, ethics, and compliance risk before the first purchase order is issued.
scope_text: This procedure applies to all NextDecade Corporation, its subsidiary Rio Grande LNG (RGLNG), and any other affiliated entity onboarding of a third-party supplier, service provider, consultant, or contractor. It does not apply to one-time de minimis purchases under the threshold defined in the Procurement Standard.
roles_intro: Roles and responsibilities for this procedure include the following.

responsibilities: Identifies the business need and the proposed vendor.
Completes the Vendor Request Form and attaches a scope of work.
Confirms no conflict of interest exists with NextDecade personnel.
Drives the vendor through the onboarding workflow.
Coordinates with HSSE, Legal, and Finance for the required assessments.
Issues the vendor activation confirmation once all approvals are on file.
Reviews the vendor's safety history, ISNetworld grade, and incident record.
Issues HSSE approval or rejection with rationale.
Runs sanctions, OFAC, and restricted-parties screening.
Approves the master services agreement or purchase terms.
Reviews the vendor's financial health and W-9/W-8.
Sets up the vendor master record in the ERP.

ppe_paragraphs: No personal protective equipment is required to execute this administrative procedure. Workforce members performing on-site vendor audits at the Rio Grande LNG facility must comply with the site-specific PPE requirements posted at the facility entrance.
Contractors performing field work on NextDecade premises are governed by the applicable site HSSE procedures and are not subject to this administrative procedure's PPE provisions.
procedure_intro: Vendor onboarding proceeds through seven sequential steps. No step may be skipped. A rejection at any step returns the vendor request to the Requestor with rationale.
steps_intro: The Supply Chain Buyer completes the following:

steps.description: Receives the Vendor Request Form from the Requestor and verifies completeness.
Sends the vendor the Vendor Qualification Questionnaire and W-9/W-8.
Routes the completed questionnaire to HSSE for safety pre-qualification review.
Routes the questionnaire and scope of work to Legal for sanctions screening and MSA negotiation.
Routes financial documentation to Finance for credit and treasury review; obtains banking setup.
Consolidates approvals into the Vendor Activation Package and obtains VP Supply Chain sign-off.
Activates the vendor in the ERP, notifies the Requestor, and files the package in the vendor master record.

recordkeeping_text: All records generated by this procedure — request forms, questionnaires, HSSE and Legal assessments, financial reviews, and final activation packages — are retained in the vendor master record for seven (7) years following vendor deactivation, consistent with ORG-NTD-000010-COR-STD-00020 Records Retention Standard and the NextDecade Procedures, Definitions and Record Keeping Requirements manual (ORG-NTD-000010-HSE-PRC-00037).
terms_intro: The following terms are specific to this document.

definitions.definition: Any third party that supplies goods or services to NextDecade under a purchase order, MSA, or statement of work.
The authoritative ERP record for a vendor, containing name, tax information, banking details, and historical activity.
The consolidated set of approvals (HSSE, Legal, Finance) required before a vendor is activated.

abbreviations_intro: The following abbreviations and acronyms are specific to this document.

abbreviations.definition: Office of Foreign Assets Control (U.S. Treasury)

references.title: Manual — Procedures, Definitions and Record Keeping Requirements

appendices.title: Vendor Qualification Questionnaire
appendices.body: The Vendor Qualification Questionnaire collects company information, ownership structure, safety history, insurance certificates, financial statements, and references. The controlled version is maintained by Supply Chain and is issued to the vendor as a fillable PDF.

appendices.title: Vendor Activation Package Checklist
appendices.body: The Vendor Activation Package includes: completed questionnaire, W-9/W-8, HSSE approval memo, Legal sanctions clearance, executed MSA or accepted PO terms, Finance credit review, banking setup confirmation, and VP Supply Chain approval signature.
